comparisons.description: Cook Now shows a list from your pantry; I Want This lets you browse, filter, and pick
Different portion sizes for each partner from the same meal, automatically optimized by AI
Instant sync between partners in under 100ms, no refreshing needed
AI suggests a list of meals based on ingredients you already have at home
Stock photo grid with filters for cook time, cuisine, difficulty, and dietary fit
Graceful equal portions when no diet is set — no forced dieting, no broken experience
Every feature built for two people sharing one kitchen, not a solo user
Works in any browser instantly — share a link and start planning
Works without internet, queues changes, syncs when reconnected
Type 'chicken, rice, spinach' and the AI understands your ingredients
Pantry → AI Meals → Shopping → Pantry — the more you use it, the smarter it gets

genericCompetitors.problem: Built for one person. Single calorie target, single portion size, single grocery list. Great if you live alone. Useless if you're cooking for two with different goals.
Know your macros but don't know your partner exists. Clinical, isolating, and ignore that cooking together should be a bonding experience, not a math equation.
Sync your shopping perfectly, but have zero idea what meals you're planning, what's in your pantry, or what your body needs. They solve one slice of the problem.
Plan for one person only. Don't understand couples, shared kitchens, or different dietary goals. They suggest meals but don't connect to your actual pantry.
Some have shared lists, some have meal planning — but none connect pantry, meal selection, shopping, and adaptive cooking into one seamless loop.

whyThisMatters.label: Wasted on duplicate groceries and expired food
whyThisMatters.detail: Without a shared system, couples buy the same things twice and forget what they already have.

whyThisMatters.label: Spent every night deciding what to cook
whyThisMatters.detail: Endless scrolling on delivery apps. Staring into the fridge. The "what's for dinner?" argument starts the stress cycle.

whyThisMatters.label: Two meals, two grocery lists, twice the cleanup
whyThisMatters.detail: When partners have different dietary goals, they cook separately. Two pans, two recipes, double the dishes.
